notes: DeepSeek's first model. Code-focused, trained on 2T tokens of code and natural language. Available in 1B, 6.7B, 33B sizes. State-of-the-art on code benchmarks at release.
General-purpose LLM series in 7B and 67B sizes. Trained on 2T tokens. Competitive with LLaMA 2 70B on reasoning and coding benchmarks.
236B MoE model with only 21B active parameters. 128K context. Outperforms GPT-4 Turbo on several benchmarks at a fraction of the inference cost.
236B MoE code model. Surpasses GPT-4 Turbo on coding benchmarks. Supports 338 programming languages, 128K context. Open-source under DeepSeek license.
Merged upgrade combining V2-Chat and V2-Coder. Improved alignment, instruction following, and writing quality. 128K context, API available.
First preview of DeepSeek's reasoning model with chain-of-thought capabilities. Available via chat interface only. Precursor to the full R1 release.
671B MoE model, 37B active parameters. Trained for just $6M. Matches GPT-4o and Claude 3.5 Sonnet on benchmarks. MIT license. Caused significant industry attention.
Open-source reasoning model matching OpenAI o1 performance. MIT license. Trained with pure RL (no supervised fine-tuning). Triggered broad industry discussion on AI cost efficiency.
Updated V3 with improved post-training using RL techniques from R1. Better reasoning, coding, and tool-use. Same base architecture as V3.
Major upgrade to R1 with stronger reasoning and inference via more compute and advanced post-training. Built on V3 base. Significantly improved math and coding scores.
Hybrid model combining V3 and R1 into one. Switches between thinking (chain-of-thought) and non-thinking modes via chat template. 671B total / 37B active params, 128K context.
Successor to V3.1 with architectural improvements. Stronger benchmark performance across reasoning, math, and coding. Open weights released alongside API.

Most capable and cost-efficient small model at release. Surpasses GPT-3.5 Turbo on academic benchmarks across textual intelligence and multimodal reasoning. Supports vision, function calling, and long-context tasks across the same languages as GPT-4o.
Open-weights models: 8B, 70B, 405B. 128K context window. First open model competitive with frontier closed models.

First preview of a new reasoning model series designed to spend more time thinking before responding. Solves harder problems in science, coding, and math than previous models. 30 messages/week rate limit at launch.
Smaller, faster counterpart to o1-preview. Cost-effective for coding and STEM tasks. 50 messages/week rate limit at launch. Paired with o1-preview as the first of OpenAI's reasoning model series.
Multimodal models (11B, 90B) with vision. Lightweight text models (1B, 3B) for edge deployment. Open weights.
Smallest Gemini model for high-frequency, low-latency tasks. 1M token context. Generally available via API.
Upgraded Sonnet with improved coding (SWE-bench 49%), vision, and computer use capability (beta).
Fastest Claude model. Matches Claude 3 Opus on many benchmarks. 200K context. API and Bedrock availability.
Full reasoning model release. Includes vision support, 200K context. Available in ChatGPT and API.
First Gemini 2.0 model. Multimodal I/O, native tool use, 1M context. Faster than 1.5 Pro. Agentic capabilities.
Updated 70B model with performance matching Llama 3.1 405B. More compute-efficient. Open weights.

Cost-efficient reasoning model optimized for coding, math, and science. Three adjustable effort levels (low/medium/high). Delivers results on par with o1 at lower latency. Expert evaluators preferred o3-mini over o1-mini 56% of the time, citing improved clarity and fewer critical errors.
First hybrid reasoning model. Switchable extended thinking mode. Top scores on SWE-bench Verified (70.3%).
Generally available release. Upgraded image generation, improved multilingual audio. 1M context. Free tier available.
Most capable Gemini 2.0 model for complex tasks. 2M context window. Code execution and Google Search grounding.

Research preview of OpenAI's largest chat model. Scales unsupervised learning to improve pattern recognition and creative insight. Feels more natural to interact with, with improved EQ, intent-following, and fewer hallucinations. Launched for Pro users; eventually expanded to all paid plans.
State-of-the-art reasoning model. 1M context, native audio/video/image understanding. Top LMArena scores.

Most powerful reasoning model. Sets new SOTA on Codeforces, SWE-bench, and MMMU. Makes 20% fewer major errors than o1 on difficult real-world tasks. Especially strong at visual tasks, programming, business analysis, and creative ideation.

Smaller reasoning model optimized for speed and cost. Best benchmark performance on AIME 2024 and 2025. Outperforms o3-mini on non-STEM tasks and data science. Supports significantly higher usage limits than o3.
MoE architecture models: Scout (17Bx16E) and Maverick (17Bx128E). Native multimodal, 10M context window.

Specialized for coding and precise instruction-following. Stronger than GPT-4o at web development tasks. Launched API-only in April 2025, then added to ChatGPT for paid users in May 2025. Same rate limits as GPT-4o.

Replaces GPT-4o mini across ChatGPT. Significant improvements in instruction-following, coding, and overall intelligence. Serves as the fallback model for free users once they reach GPT-4o usage limits.
Efficient version of Gemini 2.5 (GA). Controllable thinking budget. Best price-performance in Gemini family.
Ultra-efficient Gemini 2.5 variant optimized for speed and cost. Lowest latency in the Gemini 2.5 family.

Version of o3 that thinks longer for the most reliable responses. Expert evaluators prefer it over o3 in every tested category including science, education, programming, business, and writing. Replaced o1-pro for Pro and Team users. Supports web search, file analysis, Python, and visual reasoning.
Next generation Claude model. Enhanced coding, reasoning, and agentic task performance.
Most intelligent Claude model. Designed for complex, long-horizon tasks and deep reasoning.

Next flagship model and new default for all logged-in ChatGPT users. Combines fast and deep thinking into a single auto-switching system. Available across all tiers; Pro users get GPT-5 Thinking Pro for complex tasks.
Dedicated image generation model from Google. Better instruction following and text rendering than previous image models.
Drop-in replacement for Opus 4 with superior performance. Handles complex multi-step agentic tasks with more rigor.
Best coding model at launch with 77.2% on SWE-bench Verified. 30+ hour autonomous task horizon.
Fast and affordable Claude 4 family model. Targets latency-sensitive applications. Available on Claude.ai and mobile.

Warmer personality and improved instruction-following. Available in Instant, Thinking, and Pro variants. Retired March 11, 2026 — existing conversations automatically continued on GPT-5.3 Instant or GPT-5.4 Thinking.
Most robustly aligned Claude model. Reclaimed coding crown with 80.9% SWE-bench Verified. Significantly reduced pricing.
Best multimodal understanding model. Most powerful agentic and coding model from Google. Replaces Gemini 2.5 Pro.
Extended-thinking version of Gemini 3 Pro. Rolled out to Ultra subscribers. Designed for the hardest reasoning tasks.

Smarter and more capable GPT-5 upgrade. 400K context, 128K output. Available in Instant, Thinking, and Pro variants. Updated February 2026 for more measured, grounded tone and clearer answers to advice and how-to questions.
Agentic coding model for long-horizon task completion. Uses context compaction to work coherently across multiple context windows. 56.4% on SWE-Bench Pro, 64.0% on Terminal-Bench 2.0.
Efficient Gemini 3 model. Includes Gemini 3 Pro reasoning in a faster, cheaper package. Default model in the Gemini app.

First model combining Codex and GPT-5 training stacks. ~25% faster than GPT-5.2-Codex. Brings together best-in-class code generation, reasoning, and general intelligence. Sets new highs on SWE-Bench Pro and Terminal-Bench.
1M token context window. 14.5-hour task completion horizon. Top Finance Agent benchmark. Stronger coding and planning.
Default model for free and Pro users. 1M token context (beta). Improved computer use, coding, and large-scale data processing.
Advanced reasoning over complex problems. 77.1% on ARC-AGI-2. Available via Gemini API, Vertex AI, and NotebookLM.
Faster image generation with 4K resolution, improved text rendering, and multi-language support. #1 on Artificial Analysis Image Arena at launch.
Ultra-efficient Gemini 3.1 model for developers. Fast and cost-effective for high-volume API workloads.

Focuses on tone, relevance, and conversational flow. More accurate answers and richer web search results. Reduces unnecessary caveats and overly declarative phrasing. Updated March 16, 2026 to further reduce teaser-style phrasing.

Unifies reasoning, coding, and agentic workflows into one frontier model. Incorporates GPT-5.3-Codex capabilities. Provides upfront thinking plans in ChatGPT so users can adjust mid-response. Improved deep web research and context window management.

Available to Free and Go users via the Thinking feature. Acts as a rate limit fallback for GPT-5.4 Thinking for paid users. Not a selectable model in the picker — GPT-5 Thinking mini retired as a separate option 30 days after launch.
Smallest and cheapest OpenAI model. Optimized for classification, data extraction, ranking, and coding subagents.
